rawIntent: The CI workflow does not trigger on push to gestalt/** branches. The workflow file at .github/workflows/ci.yml is missing or does not contain the correct on.push.branches filter. The deploy orchestrator reported: 'GitHub Actions: no push-triggered run appeared on branch gestalt/2a22ec77-feature-add-a-status-module within 21s. Verify the workflow file has `on: push: branches: ['gestalt/**']` and that pr-agent's push actually landed.' This prevents automated CI from running on feature branches. Preserve all existing exports, types, interfaces, and imports. Only add or modify what is needed to resolve the CI failure shown above.

successCriteria.description: .github/workflows/ci.yml contains `on: push: branches:` with a filter that matches `gestalt/**` branches, using either YAML list syntax (`- 'gestalt/**'`) or inline array syntax (`['gestalt/**']`)
A push to any branch matching `gestalt/**` triggers the CI workflow (build-and-test job) in GitHub Actions

constraints: Preserve all existing jobs, steps, and configuration in ci.yml — only add or correct the on.push.branches trigger
The workflow file must be valid YAML that GitHub Actions can parse
No @gestalt/* packages in any generated package.json files
outOfScope: All TypeScript source files under src/
All domain modules (employee, policy, balance, leave, notification)
package.json, tsconfig.json, or any other project configuration
Any changes to application code, routes, controllers, services, or repositories
The gestalt.yml pipeline workflow — it already has the correct trigger and is not part of this fix
